title: Filter Subitems based on Status column
excerpt:
Hi there, 
I’d like to filter the Board based on the Subitem Status. The Subitems Product Map shows its Release, but couldnt find this? Please advise. 
https://view.monday.com/480751376-c22b2fe6c069076f55f20289b4d3f04f 
t…

posts.author: joexp (Joe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I’d like to filter the Board based on the Subitem Status. The Subitems Product Map shows its Release, but couldnt find this? Please advise.
https://view.monday.com/480751376-c22b2fe6c069076f55f20289b4d3f04f

thanks!

posts.author: MPUCC (mpucc)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi…
I’m also waiting for this feature longingly.
Would it be possible to get an update on this matter? In the roadmap the release date for this function is still given with date Q1/2022…

Thank you for a short update!

posts.author: rockymtnway (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: This is indeed a basic need. Items are usually Projects of Phases, and SubItems ARE the tasks! We need to filter by them!

posts.author: SConnell (Steve Connell)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m new here but see clear value in having this functionality. Any updates on ETA?

posts.author: SamiA-MB (Sami Aviles)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve gotta agree here. No filters can be currently applied using information from the subitems’ columns and it makes the subitem feature incredibly clunky. I saw there’s another post from early 2021 asking if this is possible, and it was supposedly in the pipeline for Q4 last year but there’s not been a peep about it from the Monday.com team since.

posts.author: kmcdonald (Kirsten McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: We are almost at the end of Q3 which is when this feature should be released…I am wondering if there is going to be an update on this need anytime soon?

posts.author: YaseminD (YaseminD)
posts.cookedHtml: Was about to file a feature request for this also . . . happy to see that I am not the only one, and would be great to see the update indeed.

posts.author: MPUCC (mpucc)
posts.cookedHtml: Maybe it will be announced at the ELEVATE Event as the new big feature

posts.author: JD182 (Jan Deichmann)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this function too. The Work would be much easier… Please give us an update.

posts.author: PMBrandon (Brandon Cachia)
posts.cookedHtml: Really keen to get this! Not having this makes my job quite challenging.

posts.author: zedman (Zoltan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I noticed it active in my account about 20 hours ago, so it looks like they are rolling it out.

However… it is a little odd in how it is implemented. If I create a filter:

Subitem Status is not Done

It filters out all the parent items that have at least one subitem with the status Done.

In other words if an item has two subitems (with Status Done and Working on it), the item and both subitems are hidden with this filter.

I was expecting the subitems to be filtered individually, not for the entire item to disappear if I set just one subitem to Done.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @zedman,

Thank you for this feedback, it’s really helpful for us! That said having tested this on my end, when setting up that specific filter when subitem status is not done the parent item still remains visible even if it has subitems marked both done or working on it - I’d recommend reaching out to our support team via support@monday.com if you’re still experiencing this, as I don’t believe that it’s expected

posts.author: zedman (Zoltan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this appears to be working now. It was probably fixed after the initial rollout.

I have already contacted support with other issues I noticed, so all good here.

posts.author: daniel.lim (Daniel Lim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Still awaiting for this. It’s not yet on the roadmap. Can this be added?

I just want to filter out all done items and subitems. This is still impossible to do.
